input.description: Explore Telegram plugin and plugin SDK types
input.prompt:
Explore the OpenClaw repository to understand the Telegram channel plugin structure and the plugin SDK types relevant for channel plugins.

Please:
1. Find the Telegram plugin in the bundled workspace plugins and read ALL its files (package.json, manifest, src files)
2. Read `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` completely
3. Read `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`
4. Read `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts`  
5. Read `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts`
6. Look for and read any test files (`*.test.ts`) in the Telegram or IRC plugin to understand testing patterns
7. Check `src/plugins/contracts/registry.ts` if it exists

Report every file path and its full content. I need this to understand the channel plugin contract types for implementing a new PulseBoard channel plugin.

1	# Plugin SDK Boundary
2	
3	This directory is the public contract between plugins and core. Changes here
4	can affect bundled plugins and third-party plugins.
5	
6	## Source Of Truth
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
14	- Definition files:
15	  - `package.json`
16	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
17	  - `src/plugin-sdk/entrypoints.ts`
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
22	
23	## Boundary Rules
24	
25	- Prefer narrow, purpose-built subpaths over broad convenience re-exports.
26	- Do not expose implementation convenience from `src/channels/**`,
27	  `src/agents/**`, `src/plugins/**`, or other internals unless you are
28	  intentionally promoting a supported public contract.
29	- Prefer `api.runtime` or a focused SDK facade over telling extensions to reach
30	  into host internals directly.
31	- When core or tests need bundled plugin helpers, expose them through
32	  the plugin package `api.ts` and a matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade
33	  instead of importing plugin-private `src/**` files or `onboard.js`
34	  directly.
35	
36	## Expanding The Boundary
37	
38	- Additive, backwards-compatible changes are the default.
39	- When adding or changing a public subpath, keep these aligned:
40	  - docs in `docs/plugins/*`
41	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
42	  - `src/plugin-sdk/entrypoints.ts`
43	  - `package.json` exports
44	  - API baseline and export checks
45	- If the seam is for bundled-provider onboarding/config helpers, update the
46	  generated plugin facades instead of teaching core tests or commands to reach
47	  into private extension files.
48	- Breaking removals or renames are major-version work, not drive-by cleanup.
49

1	# Channels Boundary
2	
3	`src/channels/**` is core channel implementation. Plugin authors should not
4	import from this tree directly.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	- Definition files:
13	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`
14	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts`
15	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts`
16	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
17	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
18	
19	## Boundary Rules
20	
21	- Keep extension-facing channel surfaces flowing through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`
22	  instead of direct imports from `src/channels/**`.
23	- When a bundled or third-party channel needs a new seam, add a typed SDK
24	  contract or facade first.
25	- Remember that shared channel changes affect both built-in and extension
26	  channels. Check routing, pairing, allowlists, command gating, onboarding, and
27	  reply behavior across the full set.
28

1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
86	}).superRefine((value, ctx) => {
87	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
88	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
89	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});

1	# Extensions Boundary
2	
3	This directory contains bundled plugins. Treat it as the same boundary that
4	third-party plugins see.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
14	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
15	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md`
16	  - `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
17	- Definition files:
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
22	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
23	  - `package.json`
24	
25	## Boundary Rules
26	
27	- Extension production code should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and its
28	  own local barrels such as `./api.ts` and `./runtime-api.ts`.
29	- Do not import core internals from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`,
30	  `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`, or another extension's `src/**`.
31	- Do not use relative imports that escape the current extension package root.
32	- Keep plugin metadata accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json` and the package
33	  `openclaw` block so discovery and setup work without executing plugin code.
34	- Treat files like `src/**`, `onboard.ts`, and other local helpers as private
35	  unless you intentionally promote them through `api.ts` and, if needed, a
36	  matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade.
37	- If core or core tests need a bundled plugin helper, export it from `api.ts`
38	  first instead of letting them deep-import extension internals.
39	
40	## Expanding The Boundary
41	
42	- If an extension needs a new seam, add a typed Plugin SDK subpath or additive
43	  export instead of reaching into core.
44	- Keep new plugin-facing seams backwards-compatible and versioned. Third-party
45	  plugins consume this surface.
46	- When intentionally expanding the contract, update the docs, exported subpath
47	  list, package exports, and API/contract checks in the same change.
48

30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);
98

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile my findings:

## OpenClaw Plugin System Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the OpenClaw repository to understand the plugin system architecture, channel plugins organization, and file structure. Here's my comprehensive report:

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

**Key Directories:**
- `/src/` - Core implementation (87 subdirectories)
- `/extensions/` - Bundled plugins (94 plugin directories)
- `/src/plugin-sdk/` - Public plugin contract (343 files)
- `/src/channels/` - Core channel implementation (75 files)
- `/src/plugins/` - Plugin runtime/management system
- `/packages/` - Monorepo workspaces
- `/skills/` - Skills directory
- `/docs/` - Documentation including plugin guides
- `/test/` - Test infrastructure
- `/ui/` - Frontend code
- `/Swabble/` - Swift integration

**File Paths:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775468720204-drh14y/`

### 2. **Bundled Workspace Plugin Tree**

The `/extensions/` directory contains 94 bundled plugins including:
- **Channel Plugins**: discord, slack, telegram, matrix, mattermost, irc, signal, whatsapp, line, feishu, googlechat, msteams, nextcloud-talk, tlon, zalo, bluebubbles, imessage
- **Provider Plugins**: anthropic, anthropic-vertex, amazon-bedrock, google, deepseek, openai, groq, huggingface, llama2, ollama, together, vllm, etc.
- **Specialized Plugins**: memory-core, memory-lancedb, media-understanding-core, browser, image-generation-core, etc.

Each plugin directory has the standard structure shown below.

### 3. **Key Documentation Files**

**File Paths:**
- `/src/plugin-sdk/AGENTS.md` - Plugin SDK boundary and public contract rules
- `/src/channels/AGENTS.md` - Channel implementation boundary documentation
- `/extensions/AGENTS.md` - Extensions boundary for bundled plugins
- `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
- `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
- `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
- `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`

### 4. **Plugin SDK Directory Structure**

**Path:** `/src/plugin-sdk/` (343 files)

**Core Files:**
- `channel-contract.ts` - Pure channel contract types (re-exports from channels/plugins/types.js)
- `core.ts` - Main channel plugin SDK facade with helpers (20,670 bytes)
- `plugin-entry.ts` - Plugin entry point definitions
- `provider-entry.ts` - Provider plugin definitions
- `api-baseline.ts` - API contract definitions
- `entrypoints.ts` - Entrypoint configuration

**Channel-Specific Files:**
- `channel-plugin-common.ts`
- `channel-contract.ts`
- `channel-config-helpers.ts`
- `channel-policy.ts`
- `channel-setup.ts`
- `channel-pairing.ts`
- `channel-lifecycle.ts`
- `channel-inbound.ts`
- `channel-targets.ts`
- `channel-status.ts`
- `channel-actions.ts`
- `channel-feedback.ts`

**Provider Plugin Files:**
- Individual integration files for each provider (discord.ts, slack.ts, telegram.ts, signal.ts, etc.)
- Provider auth and configuration files

### 5. **Channel Contract Types**

**File:** `/src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`

Exports these core types:
```typescript
- BaseProbeResult
- BaseTokenResolution
- ChannelAgentTool
- ChannelAccountSnapshot
- ChannelApprovalAdapter
- ChannelApprovalCapability
- ChannelCommandConversationContext
- ChannelGroupContext
- ChannelMessageActionAdapter
- ChannelMessageActionContext
- ChannelMessageActionDiscoveryContext
- ChannelMessageActionName
- ChannelMessageToolDiscovery
- ChannelMessageToolSchemaContribution
- ChannelStatusIssue
- ChannelThreadingContext
- ChannelThreadingToolContext
```

### 6. **Core SDK Types and Helpers**

**File:** `/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (585 lines)

**Key Exports:**
- `defineChannelPluginEntry()` - Main entry helper for channel plugins
- `defineSetupPluginEntry()` - Setup-only entry helper
- `createChannelPluginBase()` - Builder for channel plugin base
- `createChatChannelPlugin()` - High-level builder for chat-style channels
- `buildChannelOutboundSessionRoute()` - Session route builder
- `stripChannelTargetPrefix()` - Target parsing utility
- `stripTargetKindPrefix()` - Target kind prefix removal

**Type Definitions:**
- `ChannelPlugin<TResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>` - Full channel plugin type
- `DefineChannelPluginEntryOptions` - Entry configuration
- `ChatChannelPluginBase` - Simplified base for chat channels
- `ChatChannelSecurityOptions` - DM security builder
- `ChatChannelThreadingOptions` - Reply threading configuration

### 7. **Plugin File Structure (Example: Discord)**

**Path:** `/extensions/discord/`

**Standard Files:**
- `index.ts` - Main plugin entry using `defineChannelPluginEntry()`
- `setup-entry.ts` - Setup-only entry using `defineSetupPluginEntry()`
- `api.ts` - Public facade exporting runtime-accessible APIs
- `runtime-api.ts` - Runtime-specific exports
- `channel-config-api.ts` - Config API exports
- `test-api.ts` - Test utilities
- `session-key-api.ts` - Session key utilities
- `action-runtime-api.ts` - Action runtime exports
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - Plugin manifest
- `package.json` - NPM package definition
- `src/` - Implementation directory (120 subdirectories for Discord)
- `timeouts.ts` - Timeout constants

### 8. **Plugin Manifest Format**

**File:** `extensions/discord/openclaw.plugin.json`

```json
{
  "id": "discord",
  "channels": ["discord"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**File:** `extensions/discord/package.json`

```json
{
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "discord",
      "label": "Discord",
      "selectionLabel": "Discord (Bot API)",
      "detailLabel": "Discord Bot",
      "docsPath": "/channels/discord",
      "docsLabel": "discord",
      "blurb": "very well supported right now.",
      "systemImage": "bubble.left.and.bubble.right",
      "markdownCapable": true
    },
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/discord",
      "defaultChoice": "npm",
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "bundle": {
      "stageRuntimeDependencies": true
    },
    "release": {
      "publishToNpm": true
    }
  }
}
```

### 9. **Channel Plugin Type Hierarchy**

**File:** `/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`

**ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>** includes:
- `id`: Channel identifier
- `meta`: Channel metadata (ChannelMeta)
- `capabilities`: Supported features (ChannelCapabilities)
- `setupWizard?`: Setup UI
- `config`: Configuration adapter
- `configSchema?`: Config schema with UI hints
- `setup?`: Setup logic adapter
- `pairing?`: Approval pairing adapter
- `security?`: Security/DM policy adapter
- `groups?`: Group handling adapter
- `mentions?`: Mention handling
- `outbound?`: Message sending adapter
- `status?`: Status probing adapter
- `gateway?`: Gateway methods
- `auth?`: Authentication adapter
- `commands?`: Command handling
- `lifecycle?`: Lifecycle hooks
- `approvals?`: Approval workflows
- `allowlist?`: Allowlist handling
- `streaming?`: Streaming support
- `threading?`: Threading adapter
- `messaging?`: Messaging adapter
- `directory?`: Directory/user lookup
- `resolver?`: Custom resolver
- `actions?`: Message actions
- `heartbeat?`: Heartbeat monitoring

### 10. **Channel Adapter Contracts**

**File:** `/src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts`

Key adapter types:
- `ChannelSetupAdapter` - Account setup logic
- `ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - Account list/resolution
- `ChannelGroupAdapter` - Group-specific settings
- `ChannelOutboundAdapter` - Message sending (sendText, sendMedia, sendPoll)
- `ChannelSecurityAdapter` - DM policy and security
- `ChannelPairingAdapter` - Approval pairing flows
- `ChannelThreadingAdapter` - Reply threading modes
- `ChannelStatusAdapter` - Health/status checks
- `ChannelCommandAdapter` - Command routing
- `ChannelLifecycleAdapter` - Startup/shutdown hooks
- `ChannelAllowlistAdapter` - Allowlist enforcement
- `ChannelConfiguredBindingProvider` - Configured binding rules

### 11. **Boundary Rules**

**Plugin SDK Boundary** (`/src/plugin-sdk/AGENTS.md`):
- Prefer narrow, purpose-built subpaths over broad convenience exports
- Do not expose implementation convenience from src/channels/**, src/agents/**, src/plugins/**
- Prefer api.runtime or focused SDK facades
- Additive, backwards-compatible changes are the default
- Keep docs, scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json, src/plugin-sdk/entrypoints.ts, package.json exports aligned

**Channels Boundary** (`/src/channels/AGENTS.md`):
- Keep extension-facing surfaces through openclaw/plugin-sdk/* 
- Add typed SDK contracts/facades before new channel seams
- Check routing, pairing, allowlists, command gating across full plugin set

**Extensions Boundary** (`/extensions/AGENTS.md`):
- Import from openclaw/plugin-sdk/* and local api.ts/runtime-api.ts
- Don't import core internals or other extensions' src/**
- No relative imports escaping package root
- Treat src/**, onboard.ts as private unless promoted via api.ts

### 12. **Plugin Entry Pattern**

**Main Entry (index.ts):**
```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { discordPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setDiscordRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "discord",
  name: "Discord",
  description: "Discord channel plugin",
  plugin: discordPlugin,
  setRuntime: setDiscordRuntime,
  registerFull: registerDiscordSubagentHooks,
});
```

**Setup Entry (setup-entry.ts):**
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { discordSetupPlugin } from "./src/channel.setup.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(discordSetupPlugin);
```

### 13. **Plugin API Facade Pattern**

**api.ts** - Setup-time and public APIs:
- Account inspection helpers
- Account management
- Action handlers
- Components and UI
- Directory config
- Approvals execution
- Group policies
- Normalization utilities
- Probe definitions
- Session key normalization
- Status issue definitions
- Target parsing

**runtime-api.ts** - Runtime-specific APIs:
- Audit utilities
- Action runtime implementations
- Channel actions
- Directory live data
- Monitoring and gateway plugins
- Presence caching
- Thread bindings
- Timeouts
- User/channel resolution
- Outbound session routing
- Send implementations

This clear separation allows core to access setup-time helpers without triggering full runtime initialization.

---

**Summary:** OpenClaw uses a sophisticated plugin system with clear boundaries between plugin SDK (public contract), channels implementation (internal), and bundled extensions. Channel plugins implement a `ChannelPlugin` contract with adapters for setup, config, security, messaging, and more. The SDK provides high-level builders like `createChatChannelPlugin()` and `defineChannelPluginEntry()` that compose adapter logic. Each plugin uses api.ts and runtime-api.ts facades to control what's publicly accessible, with manifest files defining discovery and setup metadata.

## Comprehensive OpenClaw Channel Plugin Reference Implementation Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the OpenClaw repository, here's the complete analysis for creating a PulseBoard channel plugin:

---

## 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE OVERVIEW

### IRC Plugin Path
`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775468720204-drh14y/extensions/irc/`

### Nostr Plugin Path
`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775468720204-drh14y/extensions/nostr/`

---

## 2. IRC PLUGIN - COMPLETE FILE LISTING & ANALYSIS

### Root Level Files

**`/extensions/irc/package.json`**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw IRC channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "install": {
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "detailLabel": "IRC",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "docsLabel": "irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls.",
      "aliases": ["internet-relay-chat"],
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

**`/extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json`** (Minimal manifest)
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**`/extensions/irc/index.ts`**
- Entry point that exports `ircPlugin` from `./src/channel.js`
- Exports `setIrcRuntime` from `./src/runtime.js`
- Uses `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with channel plugin instance and runtime setter

**`/extensions/irc/api.ts`**
- Exports from `./src/accounts.js` and `./src/setup-surface.js`
- Thin public barrel file (boundary compliance)

**`/extensions/irc/channel-config-api.ts`**
- Exports `IrcChannelConfigSchema` from `./src/config-schema.js`

**`/extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts`**
- Simple setup entry point using `defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin)`

### Source Files (`/src/`)

**Key Architecture Files:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `channel.ts` | **CORE** - Creates the main `ChannelPlugin` object using `createChatChannelPlugin()`. Defines plugin configuration, capabilities, messaging, directory, status, gateway, pairing, security, and outbound handlers. |
| `runtime.ts` | Runtime store initialization using `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>()`. Stores the active runtime instance. |
| `runtime-api.ts` | **PRIVATE BARREL** - Re-exports SDK types from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` for internal use |
| `types.ts` | TypeScript type definitions: `IrcAccountConfig`, `IrcConfig`, `CoreConfig`, `IrcInboundMessage`, `IrcProbe` |
| `accounts.ts` | Account resolution and configuration merging. `resolveIrcAccount()` handles multi-account support, env vars, secret files, NickServ config |
| `config-schema.ts` | Zod schemas for validation: `IrcConfigSchema`, `IrcAccountSchema`, `IrcChannelConfigSchema` |
| `config-ui-hints.ts` | UI hints for configuration display |

**Protocol & Transport:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `client.ts` | `IrcClient` implementation - manages TCP/TLS sockets, handles PRIVMSG/NOTICE parsing, connection lifecycle |
| `protocol.ts` | IRC protocol parsing: message line parsing, prefix parsing, message ID generation, control char handling |
| `connect-options.ts` | Builds `IrcClientOptions` from account config (host, port, nick, auth, channels) |
| `control-chars.ts` | Detects IRC control characters (formatting codes) in messages |

**Messaging:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `send.ts` | `sendMessageIrc()` - sends text to IRC targets. Handles markdown table conversion, reply threading, transient vs persistent clients |
| `inbound.ts` | `handleIrcInbound()` - processes received IRC messages. Handles allowlist checking, pairing, group policy, command authorization, reply dispatch |
| `monitor.ts` | `monitorIrcProvider()` - long-running connection monitor. Connects client, listens for PRIVMSG, routes to inbound handler |
| `normalize.ts` | Normalizes IRC targets (#channel vs nick), allowlist entries, message targets |
| `policy.ts` | Permission/access gates: `resolveIrcGroupMatch()`, `resolveIrcGroupAccessGate()`, sender allowlist checking |
| `probe.ts` | Connection health check - quick connect/disconnect to verify IRC server reachability |

**Setup & Configuration:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `setup-core.ts` | Core setup functions: `updateIrcAccountConfig()`, `setIrcDmPolicy()`, `setIrcAllowFrom()`, `setIrcNickServ()`, `setIrcGroupAccess()` |
| `setup-surface.ts` | **CLI Wizard** - Interactive prompts for IRC setup: server details, nick, channels, allowlist, NickServ, DM policy |

**Test Files:**
- `accounts.test.ts`, `channel.test.ts`, `client.test.ts`, `config-schema.test.ts`, `connect-options.test.ts`, `control-chars.test.ts`, `inbound.*.test.ts`, `monitor.test.ts`, `normalize.test.ts`, `policy.test.ts`, `probe.test.ts`, `protocol.test.ts`, `send.test.ts`, `setup.test.ts`

---

## 3. NOSTR PLUGIN - COMPLETE FILE LISTING & ANALYSIS

### Root Level Files

**`/extensions/nostr/package.json`**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/nostr",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw Nostr channel plugin for NIP-04 encrypted DMs",
  "type": "module",
  "dependencies": {
    "nostr-tools": "^2.23.3"
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "nostr",
      "label": "Nostr",
      "selectionLabel": "Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/nostr",
      "docsLabel": "nostr",
      "blurb": "Decentralized protocol; encrypted DMs via NIP-04.",
      "order": 55,
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true
    },
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/nostr",
      "defaultChoice": "npm",
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "release": {
      "publishToNpm": true
    }
  }
}
```

**`/extensions/nostr/openclaw.plugin.json`** (Identical minimal manifest)

**`/extensions/nostr/index.ts`**
- Exports `nostrPlugin` and `setNostrRuntime`
- Implements `registerFull(api)` hook to register HTTP routes for profile sync

**`/extensions/nostr/api.ts`**
- Thin barrel: `export * from "./runtime-api.js"`

**`/extensions/nostr/runtime-api.ts`**
- Re-exports all types from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr`

**`/extensions/nostr/setup-entry.ts` & `setup-api.ts`**
- Setup entry points using `defineSetupPluginEntry(nostrPlugin)`

### Source Files (`/src/`)

**Core Channel Implementation:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `channel.ts` | Main `ChannelPlugin` using `createChatChannelPlugin()`. Implements: direct DM only (no groups), status monitoring, Nostr bus startup, message authorization (pre-crypto guard), inbound delivery with markdown support, HTTP profile sync |
| `types.ts` | Type definitions: `NostrAccountConfig`, `ResolvedNostrAccount`. Functions for account resolution and listing |
| `config-schema.ts` | Zod schemas: `NostrProfileSchema`, `NostrConfigSchema`. Profile validates NIP-01 metadata (name, displayName, about, picture, banner, website, nip05, lud16) |

**Nostr Protocol & Transport:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `nostr-bus.ts` | **CORE** - `startNostrBus()` manages Nostr relay connections, subscribes to DMs, encrypts/decrypts with NIP-04, tracks seen events, enforces guard policies, handles circuit breaker for relay failures, publishes profile (kind:0) |
| `nostr-profile.ts` | Profile publishing - converts `NostrProfile` to kind:0 event, signs, publishes to relays |
| `nostr-profile-http.ts` | HTTP handler for profile fetch/update API (GET/POST `/api/channels/nostr`) |
| `nostr-profile-import.ts` | Imports profile from relay (NIP-05 resolution, HTTP fetch) |
| `default-relays.ts` | Default relay list (damus, nos.lol, etc.) |
| `seen-tracker.ts` | Deduplication - tracks event IDs to prevent duplicate processing |
| `nostr-state-store.ts` | Persistence - reads/writes Nostr state (bus state, profile state, event history) to disk |

**Messaging & Authorization:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `session-route.ts` | Outbound session routing - prepares reply channel for Nostr messages |
| `metrics.ts` | Performance metrics collection (relay health, message counts) |

**Setup & Configuration:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `setup-surface.ts` | Interactive CLI wizard: private key entry, relay URL configuration, allowlist, DM policy |

**Test Files:**
- `channel.inbound.test.ts`, `channel.outbound.test.ts`, `channel.test.ts`, `config-schema.ts`, `default-relays.ts`, `nostr-bus.*.test.ts`, `nostr-profile*.test.ts`, `nostr-state-store.test.ts`, `seen-tracker.ts`, `session-route.ts`, `setup-surface.ts`, `test-fixtures.ts`, `types.ts`

---

## 4. KEY PLUGIN ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS

### A. Plugin Entry Pattern

**Structure:**
1. Root `index.ts` exports plugin instance + runtime setter
2. Calls `defineChannelPluginEntry({ id, name, description, plugin, setRuntime, registerFull? })`
3. `setup-entry.ts` wraps the plugin for setup wizard using `defineSetupPluginEntry()`

### B. Creating the Channel Plugin

**Core Pattern (IRC example):**
```typescript
export const ircPlugin: ChannelPlugin<ResolvedIrcAccount, IrcProbe> = createChatChannelPlugin({
  base: {
    id: "irc",
    meta: { ...channelMeta, capabilities, reload config, etc },
    config: { isConfigured, describeAccount, etc },
    groups: { resolveRequireMention, resolveToolPolicy },
    messaging: { normalizeTarget, targetResolver },
    directory: { listPeers, listGroups },
    status: { statusAdapter with probes },
    gateway: { startAccount - the long-running monitor },
  },
  pairing: { text: { notify via message } },
  security: { dmPolicy resolver, warning collector },
  outbound: { sendText, sendMedia handlers },
});
```

### C. Account Resolution Pattern

**IRC Example (`accounts.ts`):**
- `resolveIrcAccount()` merges config from:
  - Base config at `channels.irc`
  - Account-specific config at `channels.irc.accounts[id]`
  - Environment variables (IRC_HOST, IRC_NICK, IRC_PASSWORD, IRC_CHANNELS, etc.)
  - Secret files (passwordFile, NickServ passwordFile)
- Returns `ResolvedIrcAccount` with:
  - Host, port, nick, username, realname (required for configured=true)
  - Password source tracking (env/file/config/none)
  - Full IrcAccountConfig for policies, channels, groups, DMs

**Nostr Example (`types.ts`):**
- Simpler: single implicit account if privateKey present
- `resolveNostrAccount()` derives publicKey from privateKey
- Lists accounts via `listNostrAccountIds()` (empty list if no key)

### D. Configuration Schema Pattern

**Zod Schemas:**
- `*AccountSchema` - base config fields + validation rules
- `*ConfigSchema` extends AccountSchema with top-level defaults + accounts sub-object
- Use `.superRefine()` for cross-field validation
- `buildChannelConfigSchema()` converts to JSON Schema for UI

**IRC Example:**
```typescript
const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z.object({
  name: z.string().optional(),
  host: z.string().optional(),
  nick: z.string().optional(),
  channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
  groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema).optional(),
  nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
  // ... more fields
}).strict();
```

### E. Runtime Store Pattern

**Shared Pattern:**
```typescript
// runtime.ts
const { setRuntime: setIrcRuntime, getRuntime: getIrcRuntime } = 
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("IRC runtime not initialized");
export { getIrcRuntime, setIrcRuntime };
```

Access via: `getIrcRuntime().config.loadConfig()`, `getIrcRuntime().channel.activity.record()`, etc.

### F. Message Handling Pattern

**Inbound (IRC `inbound.ts`):**
1. Receive raw `IrcInboundMessage` from client
2. Check if non-empty
3. Resolve allow-from lists (config + store)
4. Resolve DM policy & access
5. Check pairing challenges if needed
6. Authorize commands
7. Dispatch reply through `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments()`

**Outbound (IRC `send.ts`):**
1. Resolve target (normalize)
2. Load markdown table mode
3. Convert markdown tables
4. Connect transient client or use existing
5. Send PRIVMSG
6. Record activity

### G. Setup Wizard Pattern

**Interactive Prompts:**
1. Status check: `createStandardChannelSetupStatus()`
2. Enable/disable channel: `setSetupChannelEnabled()`
3. DM policy: `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicy()`
4. Allowlist: `createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount()` or `createTopLevelChannelParsedAllowFromPrompt()`
5. Channel-specific config: custom prompts

**Example (IRC):**
```typescript
const promptIrcAllowFrom = createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount<CoreConfig>({
  defaultAccountId: (cfg) => resolveDefaultIrcAccountId(cfg),
  noteTitle: "IRC allowlist",
  noteLines: ["Allowlist IRC DMs by sender.", "- alice", "- alice!ident@example.org"],
  message: "IRC allowFrom (nick or nick!user@host)",
  placeholder: "alice, bob!ident@example.org",
  parseEntries: (raw) => ({ entries: parseListInput(raw).map(normalizeIrcAllowEntry).filter(Boolean) }),
  getExistingAllowFrom: ({ cfg }) => cfg.channels?.irc?.allowFrom ?? [],
  applyAllowFrom: ({ cfg, allowFrom }) => setIrcAllowFrom(cfg, allowFrom),
});
```

### H. Long-Running Monitor Pattern

**Gateway Start (`monitor.ts`):**
1. Resolve account from config
2. Get runtime logger
3. Create client connection with callbacks
4. Listen for onPrivmsg events
5. Call `handleIrcInbound()` for each message
6. Return `{ stop: () => void }`

---

## 5. CRITICAL INTERFACES & CONTRACTS

### ChannelPlugin Structure
```typescript
interface ChannelPlugin<Account, Probe = never> {
  base: {
    id: string;
    meta: ChannelMeta;
    setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter;
    setupWizard?: ChannelSetupWizard;
    capabilities: { chatTypes: ("direct" | "group")[]; media: boolean; blockStreaming?: boolean };
    reload: { configPrefixes: string[] };
    configSchema: JSONSchema;
    config: {
      isConfigured: (account: Account) => boolean;
      describeAccount: (account: Account) => string;
      listAccountIds: (cfg) => string[];
      resolveDefaultAccountId: (cfg) => string;
      resolveAccount: (cfg, accountId?) => Account;
      // ... more
    };
    messaging: {
      normalizeTarget: (raw: string) => string | undefined;
      targetResolver: { looksLikeId, hint };
      resolveOutboundSessionRoute?: (params) => SessionRoute;
    };
    directory?: {
      listPeers: (params) => Promise<DirectoryEntry[]>;
      listGroups: (params) => Promise<DirectoryEntry[]>;
    };
    status?: {
      // ... status resolution
    };
    gateway: {
      startAccount: (ctx: GatewayStartContext) => Promise<void>;
    };
  };
  pairing?: {
    text: {
      idLabel: string;
      message: string;
      normalizeAllowEntry: (entry: string) => string | null;
      notify: (params) => Promise<void>;
    };
  };
  security: {
    resolveDmPolicy?: (account: Account) => Promise<DmPolicy>;
    collectWarnings?: (accounts: Account[]) => string[];
  };
  outbound: {
    base: {
      deliveryMode: "direct" | "batch";
      chunker?: (text: string, limit: number) => string[];
      chunkerMode?: "markdown" | "newline";
      textChunkLimit?: number;
    };
    attachedResults?: {
      channel: string;
      sendText: (params) => Promise<void>;
      sendMedia: (params) => Promise<void>;
    };
  };
}
```

### Account Configuration Pattern
- Config always nested under `channels.{id}` in OpenClaw config
- Multi-account support via `channels.{id}.accounts[accountId]`
- Account resolution merges base config + account-specific + env vars
- Returns `Resolved*Account` type with derived/validated fields

---

## 6. DISCORD PLUGIN REFERENCE (COMPARISON)

**Path:** `/extensions/discord/`

**Key Differences:**
- **More Complex:** Has action runtime API, subagent hooks, component handling, voice support
- **Structure:** Similar root pattern (index.ts, setup-entry.ts, package.json)
- **Scale:** 100+ files vs IRC's 30+ files (indicates complexity level)
- **Capabilities:** `chatTypes: ["direct", "group"]`, `media: true`, markdown formatting

**Use Discord as reference for:**
- Multi-capability implementation
- Complex state management
- Integration hooks (registerFull)

---

## 7. PLUGIN SDK IMPORTS & BOUNDARIES

**Key SDK Imports:**
```typescript
// Core entry
import { defineChannelPluginEntry, createChatChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";

// Config & accounts
import { createAccountListHelpers, resolveMergedAccountConfig } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers";
import { createScopedChannelConfigAdapter } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers";

// Validation
import { z, buildChannelConfigSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";

// Message delivery
import { deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments, dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc"; // or nostr

// Setup wizards
import { createStandardChannelSetupStatus, createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup";

// Status & monitoring
import { createComputedAccountStatusAdapter, createDefaultChannelRuntimeState } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers";

// Runtime
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";
```

**Boundary Rules:**
- Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` only
- Use local `api.ts` and `runtime-api.ts` for re-exports
- Don't import from `src/**` of other plugins
- Don't use relative imports escaping package root

---

## 8. IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST FOR PULSEBOARD

Based on the IRC pattern (simpler than Nostr), implement:

1. **Root Files:**
   - [ ] `package.json` with `openclaw.channel` config
   - [ ] `openclaw.plugin.json` (minimal)
   - [ ] `index.ts` - entry point with `defineChannelPluginEntry()`
   - [ ] `setup-entry.ts` - setup wizard entry
   - [ ] `api.ts` - public barrel exports
   - [ ] `channel-config-api.ts` - export config schema

2. **Types (`src/types.ts`):**
   - [ ] `PulseBoardAccountConfig` interface
   - [ ] `CoreConfig` with `channels.pulseboard`
   - [ ] `PulseBoardInboundMessage` (what the API returns)
   - [ ] `PulseBoardProbe` for health checks

3. **Account Resolution (`src/accounts.ts`):**
   - [ ] `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` type
   - [ ] `resolvePulseBoardAccount()` function
   - [ ] Merge config + env vars + secret files
   - [ ] `listPulseBoardAccountIds()`, `resolveDefaultPulseBoardAccountId()`

4. **Configuration (`src/config-schema.ts`):**
   - [ ] Zod schemas: `PulseBoardAccountSchema`, `PulseBoardConfigSchema`
   - [ ] Use `buildChannelConfigSchema()` for JSON Schema export

5. **Transport (`src/client.ts`):**
   - [ ] Connect to PulseBoard API/WebSocket
   - [ ] Implement message listening loop
   - [ ] Handle authentication/API key management
   - [ ] Manage connection lifecycle

6. **Messaging:**
   - [ ] `src/send.ts` - `sendMessagePulseBoard()`
   - [ ] `src/inbound.ts` - `handlePulseBoardInbound()`
   - [ ] `src/monitor.ts` - `monitorPulseBoardProvider()`
   - [ ] `src/normalize.ts` - normalize targets/allowlist entries

7. **Plugin Definition (`src/channel.ts`):**
   - [ ] Call `createChatChannelPlugin()` with base config
   - [ ] Implement base, pairing, security, outbound sections

8. **Setup (`src/setup-core.ts`, `src/setup-surface.ts`):**
   - [ ] Configuration update functions
   - [ ] Interactive wizard prompts

9. **Testing:**
   - [ ] Unit tests for accounts, config, normalize, protocol
   - [ ] Integration tests for inbound/outbound
   - [ ] Setup wizard tests

---

## 9. FILE PATH SUMMARY - ALL KEY FILES

### Nostr Plugin (Advanced Reference)

---

## 10. EXECUTION FLOW EXAMPLES

### IRC Inbound Message Flow
1. `monitorIrcProvider()` starts long-running connection
2. Client emits `onPrivmsg` event
3. Wraps in `IrcInboundMessage` (target, senderNick, text, timestamp)
4. Calls `handleIrcInbound()` with message + account + config + runtime
5. Handler checks allowlist, resolves DM policy, checks pairing
6. If allowed: calls `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase()` or `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
7. Delivers formatted reply through `deliver` callback
8. Reply text formatted (markdown→IRC), chunked (350 char limit), sent via `sendMessageIrc()`

### Nostr Inbound Message Flow
1. `startNostrBus()` starts relay connections + subscribes to DMs
2. Relay emits encrypted kind:4 event
3. Pre-crypto guard validates sender (before decrypting)
4. Decrypts using NIP-04
5. Calls `onMessage()` callback with pubkey + text + reply fn
6. Dispatches via `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
7. Reply callback sends encrypted kind:4 back to relay

---

This comprehensive guide covers all essential patterns, file structures, and implementation requirements for creating a PulseBoard channel plugin following OpenClaw conventions.

## 4. KEY PLUGIN ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS

### A. Plugin Entry Pattern

**Structure:**
1. Root `index.ts` exports plugin instance + runtime setter
2. Calls `defineChannelPluginEntry({ id, name, description, plugin, setRuntime, registerFull? })`
3. `setup-entry.ts` wraps the plugin for setup wizard using `defineSetupPluginEntry()`

### B. Creating the Channel Plugin

**Core Pattern (IRC example):**
```typescript
export const ircPlugin: ChannelPlugin<ResolvedIrcAccount, IrcProbe> = createChatChannelPlugin({
  base: {
    id: "irc",
    meta: { ...channelMeta, capabilities, reload config, etc },
    config: { isConfigured, describeAccount, etc },
    groups: { resolveRequireMention, resolveToolPolicy },
    messaging: { normalizeTarget, targetResolver },
    directory: { listPeers, listGroups },
    status: { statusAdapter with probes },
    gateway: { startAccount - the long-running monitor },
  },
  pairing: { text: { notify via message } },
  security: { dmPolicy resolver, warning collector },
  outbound: { sendText, sendMedia handlers },
});
```

### C. Account Resolution Pattern

**IRC Example (`accounts.ts`):**
- `resolveIrcAccount()` merges config from:
  - Base config at `channels.irc`
  - Account-specific config at `channels.irc.accounts[id]`
  - Environment variables (IRC_HOST, IRC_NICK, IRC_PASSWORD, IRC_CHANNELS, etc.)
  - Secret files (passwordFile, NickServ passwordFile)
- Returns `ResolvedIrcAccount` with:
  - Host, port, nick, username, realname (required for configured=true)
  - Password source tracking (env/file/config/none)
  - Full IrcAccountConfig for policies, channels, groups, DMs

**Nostr Example (`types.ts`):**
- Simpler: single implicit account if privateKey present
- `resolveNostrAccount()` derives publicKey from privateKey
- Lists accounts via `listNostrAccountIds()` (empty list if no key)

### D. Configuration Schema Pattern

**Zod Schemas:**
- `*AccountSchema` - base config fields + validation rules
- `*ConfigSchema` extends AccountSchema with top-level defaults + accounts sub-object
- Use `.superRefine()` for cross-field validation
- `buildChannelConfigSchema()` converts to JSON Schema for UI

**IRC Example:**
```typescript
const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z.object({
  name: z.string().optional(),
  host: z.string().optional(),
  nick: z.string().optional(),
  channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
  groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema).optional(),
  nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
  // ... more fields
}).strict();
```

### E. Runtime Store Pattern

**Shared Pattern:**
```typescript
// runtime.ts
const { setRuntime: setIrcRuntime, getRuntime: getIrcRuntime } = 
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("IRC runtime not initialized");
export { getIrcRuntime, setIrcRuntime };
```

Access via: `getIrcRuntime().config.loadConfig()`, `getIrcRuntime().channel.activity.record()`, etc.

### F. Message Handling Pattern

**Inbound (IRC `inbound.ts`):**
1. Receive raw `IrcInboundMessage` from client
2. Check if non-empty
3. Resolve allow-from lists (config + store)
4. Resolve DM policy & access
5. Check pairing challenges if needed
6. Authorize commands
7. Dispatch reply through `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments()`

**Outbound (IRC `send.ts`):**
1. Resolve target (normalize)
2. Load markdown table mode
3. Convert markdown tables
4. Connect transient client or use existing
5. Send PRIVMSG
6. Record activity

### G. Setup Wizard Pattern

**Interactive Prompts:**
1. Status check: `createStandardChannelSetupStatus()`
2. Enable/disable channel: `setSetupChannelEnabled()`
3. DM policy: `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicy()`
4. Allowlist: `createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount()` or `createTopLevelChannelParsedAllowFromPrompt()`
5. Channel-specific config: custom prompts

**Example (IRC):**
```typescript
const promptIrcAllowFrom = createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount<CoreConfig>({
  defaultAccountId: (cfg) => resolveDefaultIrcAccountId(cfg),
  noteTitle: "IRC allowlist",
  noteLines: ["Allowlist IRC DMs by sender.", "- alice", "- alice!ident@example.org"],
  message: "IRC allowFrom (nick or nick!user@host)",
  placeholder: "alice, bob!ident@example.org",
  parseEntries: (raw) => ({ entries: parseListInput(raw).map(normalizeIrcAllowEntry).filter(Boolean) }),
  getExistingAllowFrom: ({ cfg }) => cfg.channels?.irc?.allowFrom ?? [],
  applyAllowFrom: ({ cfg, allowFrom }) => setIrcAllowFrom(cfg, allowFrom),
});
```

### H. Long-Running Monitor Pattern

**Gateway Start (`monitor.ts`):**
1. Resolve account from config
2. Get runtime logger
3. Create client connection with callbacks
4. Listen for onPrivmsg events
5. Call `handleIrcInbound()` for each message
6. Return `{ stop: () => void }`

## 5. CRITICAL INTERFACES & CONTRACTS

### ChannelPlugin Structure
```typescript
interface ChannelPlugin<Account, Probe = never> {
  base: {
    id: string;
    meta: ChannelMeta;
    setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter;
    setupWizard?: ChannelSetupWizard;
    capabilities: { chatTypes: ("direct" | "group")[]; media: boolean; blockStreaming?: boolean };
    reload: { configPrefixes: string[] };
    configSchema: JSONSchema;
    config: {
      isConfigured: (account: Account) => boolean;
      describeAccount: (account: Account) => string;
      listAccountIds: (cfg) => string[];
      resolveDefaultAccountId: (cfg) => string;
      resolveAccount: (cfg, accountId?) => Account;
      // ... more
    };
    messaging: {
      normalizeTarget: (raw: string) => string | undefined;
      targetResolver: { looksLikeId, hint };
      resolveOutboundSessionRoute?: (params) => SessionRoute;
    };
    directory?: {
      listPeers: (params) => Promise<DirectoryEntry[]>;
      listGroups: (params) => Promise<DirectoryEntry[]>;
    };
    status?: {
      // ... status resolution
    };
    gateway: {
      startAccount: (ctx: GatewayStartContext) => Promise<void>;
    };
  };
  pairing?: {
    text: {
      idLabel: string;
      message: string;
      normalizeAllowEntry: (entry: string) => string | null;
      notify: (params) => Promise<void>;
    };
  };
  security: {
    resolveDmPolicy?: (account: Account) => Promise<DmPolicy>;
    collectWarnings?: (accounts: Account[]) => string[];
  };
  outbound: {
    base: {
      deliveryMode: "direct" | "batch";
      chunker?: (text: string, limit: number) => string[];
      chunkerMode?: "markdown" | "newline";
      textChunkLimit?: number;
    };
    attachedResults?: {
      channel: string;
      sendText: (params) => Promise<void>;
      sendMedia: (params) => Promise<void>;
    };
  };
}
```

### Account Configuration Pattern
- Config always nested under `channels.{id}` in OpenClaw config
- Multi-account support via `channels.{id}.accounts[accountId]`
- Account resolution merges base config + account-specific + env vars
- Returns `Resolved*Account` type with derived/validated fields

**Boundary Rules:**
- Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` only
- Use local `api.ts` and `runtime-api.ts` for re-exports
- Don't import from `src/**` of other plugins
- Don't use relative imports escaping package root

## 10. EXECUTION FLOW EXAMPLES

### IRC Inbound Message Flow
1. `monitorIrcProvider()` starts long-running connection
2. Client emits `onPrivmsg` event
3. Wraps in `IrcInboundMessage` (target, senderNick, text, timestamp)
4. Calls `handleIrcInbound()` with message + account + config + runtime
5. Handler checks allowlist, resolves DM policy, checks pairing
6. If allowed: calls `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase()` or `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
7. Delivers formatted reply through `deliver` callback
8. Reply text formatted (markdown→IRC), chunked (350 char limit), sent via `sendMessageIrc()`

### Nostr Inbound Message Flow
1. `startNostrBus()` starts relay connections + subscribes to DMs
2. Relay emits encrypted kind:4 event
3. Pre-crypto guard validates sender (before decrypting)
4. Decrypts using NIP-04
5. Calls `onMessage()` callback with pubkey + text + reply fn
6. Dispatches via `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
7. Reply callback sends encrypted kind:4 back to relay

  - `outbound.sendMedia()`, `outbound.sendPayload()`

- Testing with mocked dependencies

**IRC Plugin Test File (channel.test.ts)**

Simpler test example:

### 7. Boundary Documentation

**Extensions Boundary**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775468720204-drh14y/extensions/CLAUDE.md`

Rules for extension development:
- Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and local `api.ts`/`runtime-api.ts`

## Key Takeaways for Implementing a PulseBoard Channel Plugin

1. **Entry Point**: Create an `index.ts` that exports `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with your channel plugin
2. **Plugin Object**: Implement a `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount>` object with required and optional adapters
3. **Config Adapter**: Required `ChannelConfigAdapter` with `listAccountIds()` and `resolveAccount()`
4. **Outbound Adapter**: Implement `ChannelOutboundAdapter` with send methods
5. **Status Adapter**: Optional but recommended for health checks and diagnostics
6. **Gateway Adapter**: For account lifecycle management (start/stop)
7. **Manifest**: Create `openclaw.plugin.json` with channel metadata
8. **Package.json**: Include `openclaw` block with extensions, setupEntry, and channel metadata
9. **Testing**: Use vitest with mocking patterns shown in Telegram tests
10. **Type Safety**: Leverage the plugin SDK types from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-contract`

### 2. Core Channel Plugin Type Definitions

**Channel Plugin Type (Main Contract)**

This file exports the comprehensive `ChannelPlugin` type that all channel plugins must implement. Key sections:

- `ChannelConfigUiHint`: UI hints for config fields (label, help, tags, advanced, sensitive, placeholder, itemTemplate)
- `ChannelConfigRuntimeIssue`: Config validation issues with path, message, and code
- `ChannelConfigRuntimeParseResult`: Union type for successful or failed config parsing
- `ChannelConfigRuntimeSchema`: Schema with safeParse method for runtime validation
- `ChannelConfigSchema`: Published config schema with JSON schema and UI hints
- `ChannelPlugin` (main type): Full capability contract including:

  - `meta`: Metadata (label, docs path, blurb, etc.)

  - `config`: Config adapter

  - `groups`, `mentions`, `outbound`: Message and routing adapters
  - `status`, `gateway`, `auth`: Runtime adapters
  - `approvalCapability`, `elevated`, `commands`: Approval and authorization
  - `lifecycle`, `approvals`, `allowlist`: Lifecycle and security
  - `bindings`, `conversationBindings`: Session routing
  - `streaming`, `threading`, `messaging`: Message handling
  - `agentPrompt`, `directory`, `resolver`, `actions`, `heartbeat`: Agent interaction
  - `agentTools`: Channel-owned agent tools

- `ChannelMessageActionDiscoveryContext`: Discovery context for actions
- `ChannelMessageToolSchemaContribution`: Schema fragments with visibility
- `ChannelMessageToolDiscovery`: Action and capability discovery
- `ChannelSetupInput`: Unified setup input bag
- `ChannelStatusIssue`: Status problem representation
- `ChannelAccountState`: States: linked, configured, enabled, etc.
- `ChannelHeartbeatDeps`: Dependencies for heartbeat checks
- `ChannelMeta`: User-facing metadata
- `ChannelAccountSnapshot`: Runtime account state snapshot
- `ChannelLogSink`: Logging interface

1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,

## Plan Overview

The PulseBoard is a fictional real-time bulletin board messaging platform where:
- Users post messages to named "boards" (groups)
- Users can also send direct messages (DMs)
- Supports both direct and group chat types
- Has a 4000-character text chunk limit

I need to create a new extension at `extensions/pulseboard/` following the IRC plugin pattern (simpler than Telegram/Discord).


I'm setting up the directory structure with the core plugin files—package metadata, the plugin manifest, entry points for the main plugin and setup wizard, public API exports, and type definitions along with account resolution logic.

For PulseBoard specifically, I'm designing a fictional platform that uses `@username` for direct messages and `#boardname` for group posts, connecting through HTTP with an API key and optional server URL configuration.

The config needs to support server connection details, authentication, board membership, user identity settings, access policies for both DMs and boards, and standard messaging parameters like chunk limits. I'll also need a health check probe and inbound message handling that tracks message IDs and board context.

For message IDs, I'm using a simple timestamp-based format with random suffixes. Target normalization handles both board and DM addressing conventions, converting various PulseBoard formats to standard IRC-style notation.

Now I'm thinking through the runtime architecture—since there's no dedicated PulseBoard SDK, I'll need to reuse the IRC runtime helpers as the foundation. Now I'm laying out the comprehensive plan for PulseBoard with the channel ID, supported chat types, text limits, and configuration fields like server URL, API key, and user identifier.

I'm also defining the per-board configuration structure with settings for mention requirements, tool policies, skills, and system prompts, then mapping the group and DM formats using hashtags for boards and @ mentions for direct messages.

For the client layer, I'm creating a mock PulseBoardClient that simulates HTTP API interactions with connection management, message sending, and a polling mechanism to receive messages, plus a quick health check probe to verify server connectivity and authentication.

- Supports both direct and group chat types

The constraint is that I can only create new files in the pulseboard extension, so I'll need to import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` in the `runtime-api.ts` file since it's already a public subpath listed in `plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json` and contains the generic channel helpers I need. The gateway starts a long-running monitor for the account, while the pairing section handles text-based pairing with a message notification system that sends approval messages through PulseBoard, and finally I'm defining security policies for direct message handling.

Configuring outbound messaging with a 4000-character chunk limit to respect PulseBoard's constraints, then setting up handlers for both text and media attachments that format and send messages through the PulseBoard API.

For monitoring and health checks, I'm setting up a WebSocket/polling connection that feeds incoming messages into the inbound handler, plus a quick HTTP probe to verify the API is reachable. The setup functions follow the same configuration pattern as IRC, managing account settings, DM policies, and group access controls.

I'm deciding between the group+DM dispatcher pattern from the IRC SDK versus the simpler DM-only approach, and since PulseBoard handles both, I'll go with the more flexible group+DM pattern. Here's my complete plan:

---

## Context

The goal is to add a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin to OpenClaw. PulseBoard is an imaginary real-time bulletin board messaging platform where users post messages to named "boards" and can send direct messages. The plugin must follow the same extension structure as existing channel plugins (IRC, Nostr, Telegram) and meet these requirements:


- Supports both `direct` and `group` chat types
- 4000-character text chunk limit
- All new files created ONLY in `extensions/pulseboard/`
- No modifications to existing files

## Files to Create

I need to set up the root-level entry files in `extensions/pulseboard/`: a package.json with OpenClaw channel metadata, a minimal plugin manifest, the main plugin entry point using the channel plugin definition, and a setup wizard entry.

Then I'll create the public API barrel exports that expose account resolution and setup surface functionality, along with the channel config schema export.

For the implementation layer in `src/`, I'm defining the core type system for accounts, board configurations, and inbound messages, then building out the account resolution logic with functions to list and resolve accounts, and finally the config schema validation.

I'm also setting up UI hints for configuration fields, a runtime store for plugin state, and helper functions for normalizing messaging targets and checking board identifiers.

The policy layer handles group access control with functions for matching groups and resolving access gates and mention requirements.

Then there's the client implementation with a simulated PulseBoard connection, connectivity probes, message sending via HTTP, the full inbound message dispatch pipeline, a long-running connection monitor, and core setup functions for managing account configuration and DM policies.

The surface-level setup provides an interactive wizard for channel configuration, and the main plugin ties everything together as a ChannelPlugin that integrates with the broader chat system.

I've also outlined test coverage for the outbound message chunking logic and target normalization, and now I'm ready to write out the complete implementation plan.

content.text:
Here is the plan I've put together after a thorough read of the IRC, Nostr, and Telegram plugins, as well as the plugin SDK contracts.

---

## Plan: `extensions/pulseboard/` — 20 new files, zero existing files touched

### What was explored

- All files in `extensions/irc/` (the primary reference — closest match to PulseBoard's group + DM + API-key pattern)
- `extensions/nostr/` and `extensions/telegram/` for comparison
- `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/irc.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/direct-dm.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/channel-reply-pipeline.ts` and the full `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
- All adapter type definitions in `src/channels/plugins/types.*.ts`

### Key decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| IRC as the reference | Only plugin with group + DM + API-key style auth; no heavy 3rd-party SDK |
| `runtime-api.ts` imports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` | That surface is listed in the public entrypoints JSON and contains all generic channel helpers (`dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `createChannelPairingController`, etc.) that a multi-chat-type plugin needs. No `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` can be created under the no-modify constraint. |
| `textChunkLimit: 4000` | PulseBoard's stated platform limit |
| `media: false` | PulseBoard is text-only (bulletin boards) |
| Target syntax | `board:<name>` / `#board-name` for groups; `user:<id>` for DMs |
| Env vars | `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_BOT_USER_ID`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL` |
| Fictional transport | `setInterval` + `fetch` — no npm dependency needed |

### File count: 20 new files

6 root files + 14 `src/` files, all inside `extensions/pulseboard/`.

### Verification gates

```
pnpm tsgo          # type-check
pnpm check         # lint + format
pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard/src/   # scoped tests
pnpm test          # full suite (regression check)
pnpm build         # module boundary / dynamic-import check
```

| `runtime-api.ts` imports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` | That surface is listed in the public entrypoints JSON and contains all generic channel helpers (`dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `createChannelPairingController`, etc.) that a multi-chat-type plugin needs. No `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` can be created under the no-modify constraint. |
| `textChunkLimit: 4000` | PulseBoard's stated platform limit |
| `media: false` | PulseBoard is text-only (bulletin boards) |
| Target syntax | `board:<name>` / `#board-name` for groups; `user:<id>` for DMs |
| Env vars | `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_BOT_USER_ID`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL` |
| Fictional transport | `setInterval` + `fetch` — no npm dependency needed |

6 root files + 14 `src/` files, all inside `extensions/pulseboard/`.

```

15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({

92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',

// Private runtime barrel for the bundled IRC extension.
// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.

export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  isDangerousNameMatchingEnabled,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  BlockStreamingCoalesceConfig,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmConfig,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  GroupToolPolicyBySenderConfig,
  GroupToolPolicyConfig,
  MarkdownConfig,
  OpenClawConfig,
  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

Here's my plan for the `extensions/pulseboard/` directory:

**Root files:**

3. `index.ts` - Main plugin entry point

**Source files (src/):**

3. `config-ui-hints.ts` - UI hints
4. `runtime-api.ts` - Runtime API barrel (re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`)

6. `accounts.ts` - Account management
7. `normalize.ts` - Normalization utilities
8. `policy.ts` - Policy enforcement (mention gate, group access gate)
9. `inbound.ts` - Inbound message handling
10. `send.ts` - Message sending
11. `client.ts` - PulseBoard "client" (fictional transport)
12. `monitor.ts` - Provider monitoring
13. `probe.ts` - Connectivity probing
14. `setup-core.ts` - Core setup configuration
15. `setup-surface.ts` - Setup wizard
16. `channel.ts` - Main channel plugin

**Test files:**
1. `src/accounts.test.ts`
2. `src/normalize.test.ts`
3. `src/config-schema.test.ts`
4. `src/channel.test.ts`

Now I'm laying out the core design for PulseBoard, starting with the channel ID and environment variables I'll need for API authentication and server connectivity.

Setting up the package.json... I'm setting up the main entry point for the PulseBoard plugin, importing the channel definition and runtime setup, then exporting them along with a plugin entry that ties everything together with the appropriate type definitions and configuration. Defining the configuration types for PulseBoard accounts and boards, which specify settings like API credentials, policies for tool access, messaging rules, and board-specific configurations. Defining the message and probe types... Continuing with the type exports and then setting up the runtime store and account management modules... Setting up account list helpers for Pulseboard and creating a function to merge account configuration from the core config with account-specific overrides. Writing the account resolution logic... Now I'm looking at how to resolve the fallback Pulseboard account when the primary one isn't configured, then listing all enabled accounts by mapping over the account IDs and filtering for those that are active. After that, I'm defining the normalization patterns for Pulseboard targets—boards use a specific format like `board:<name>` or `#board-name`, while direct messages target users with `user:<id>` or just a plain ID.

Normalizing messaging targets... Writing utility functions... Writing the board matching logic... Now I'm checking the allowlist policy logic—if there are no configured boards or the board isn't allowlisted, I return denied access, and then I verify that neither the specific board config nor the wildcard config has been disabled before allowing access based on the policy type.

I'm also defining a function to resolve whether mentions are required by checking the board config first, then the wildcard config, defaulting to true if neither specifies a value.

Writing mention gate logic... Finishing the allowlist resolution logic and then moving into the client implementation with a fictional fetch-based polling transport for the PulseBoard API. Writing the client connection logic... Writing the polling logic...

Now I'm setting up the client object with a send method that posts messages to the API, handling the response and generating a message ID if one isn't returned.

I'm also adding a stop method that clears the interval and sets a stopped flag, then returning the client. After that, I'm defining a probe function that measures latency by sending a GET request to the server's ping endpoint with a timeout and calculating how long it took.

Now I'm setting up the probe module itself, which resolves the account configuration and prepares to test the connection. I'm setting up the send functionality by importing the necessary utilities and defining the result type, then creating a helper function that records outbound activity to the Pulseboard runtime while gracefully handling cases where the runtime isn't initialized.

23 files total. Let me do a quick sanity check on some of the key files to make sure they're correct, and then verify there are no obvious issues.

Let me check:
1. That `monitor.ts` doesn't use the `await import("./send.js")` dynamic import in a way that violates the dynamic import guardrail.

The CLAUDE.md says:
> Dynamic import guardrail: do not mix await import("x") and static import ... from "x" for the same module in production code paths. If you need lazy loading, create a dedicated *.runtime.ts boundary (that re-exports from x) and dynamically import that boundary from lazy callers only.


In `monitor.ts`, I'm using a dynamic import of `./send.js` inside the sendReply callback, but the IRC version uses a direct method call instead. This creates an inconsistency where I'm mixing dynamic imports with what should be a static pattern, which violates the guardrail. file, I could have both a static and dynamic import. But actually I don't have a static import of `./send.js` in `monitor.ts`.

Actually, looking more carefully at the guardrail:
> do not mix await import("x") and static import ... from "x" for the same module in production code paths.

Since I only have a dynamic import (no static import) of `./send.js` in `monitor.ts`, this should be fine per the rule. The rule is about mixing static and dynamic, not about dynamic alone.

But wait - there's also the [INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT] warning to check for. Since `send.ts` is already imported statically in `inbound.ts`, and `inbound.ts` is statically imported by `monitor.ts`... hmm, it gets complex.

Actually, let me trace through the actual dependency structure. In IRC, `monitor.ts` imports `inbound.ts` statically and uses the client for sending, so there's no circular dependency there. For PulseBoard, `monitor.ts` calls `handlePulseboardInbound` from `inbound.ts` and needs to provide a `sendReply` callback that calls `sendMessagePulseboard`. The issue is that `inbound.ts` already imports `send.ts` statically, so the dynamic import in `monitor.ts` might be unnecessary.

Let me just convert the dynamic import to a static import at the top of `monitor.ts` and pass `sendMessagePulseboard` directly to the callback. This is cleaner and avoids any potential issues.

## `extensions/pulseboard/` — 23 new files, zero existing files modified

### Root files (6)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata; declares channel id `pulseboard`, label `PulseBoard`, setup entry |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest: id `pulseboard`, channel `pulseboard` |
| `index.ts` | `defineChannelPluginEntry` wiring for `pulseboardPlugin` + `setPulseboardRuntime` |
| `api.ts` | Public barrel: re-exports accounts + setup-surface |
| `channel-config-api.ts` | Exports `PulseboardChannelConfigSchema` for external consumers |
| `setup-entry.ts` | `defineSetupPluginEntry` for the setup wizard flow |

### `src/` files (17, including 3 test files)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `types.ts` | All TypeScript types: `PulseboardAccountConfig`, `PulseboardConfig`, `CoreConfig`, `PulseboardInboundMessage`, `PulseboardProbe`, `PulseboardBoardConfig` |
| `runtime-api.ts` | Private barrel re-exporting shared channel helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` |
| `runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore` wrapper: `getPulseboardRuntime` / `setPulseboardRuntime` |
| `accounts.ts` | `resolvePulseboardAccount`, multi-account list helpers; reads `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_BOT_USER_ID` env vars |
| `normalize.ts` | Target normalization (`board:general` → `#general`, `user:alice` → `alice`), allowlist matching |
| `config-schema.ts` | Zod schema with `PulseboardConfigSchema` and `PulseboardChannelConfigSchema`; `pollIntervalMs ≥ 500` guard |
| `config-ui-hints.ts` | `ChannelConfigUiHint` map for all PulseBoard config fields |
| `policy.ts` | Group-match, access-gate, mention-gate, sender-allowed — all board-aware policy logic |
| `channel.ts` | `pulseboardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin`; `textChunkLimit: 4000`, `media: false`, directory/status/gateway/pairing/security/outbound wired up |
| `inbound.ts` | `handlePulseboardInbound`: full dm-policy + group-policy + mention-gate + pairing + dispatch pipeline |
| `send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseboard`: fictional HTTP `POST /boards/{id}/messages` or `POST /dm/{userId}` with Bearer auth |
| `monitor.ts` | Polling loop (`setInterval` + `fetch /messages?since=…`), `probePulseboard` (`GET /health`) |
| `setup-core.ts` | `pulseboardSetupAdapter`, config patch helpers, `setPulseboardDmPolicy/AllowFrom/GroupAccess` |
| `setup-surface.ts` | `pulseboardSetupWizard`: full wizard flow with env-var detection, board subscriptions, group-access, allowFrom, finalize |
| `accounts.test.ts` | Tests for account list, default resolution, config/env API key sources |
| `normalize.test.ts` | Tests for target normalization, allowlist matching, name-matching opt-in |
| `policy.test.ts` | Tests for group-match, access-gate, mention-gate, sender-allowed logic |
